raw_response:
I agree with the assessment provided in the previous discussion. The primary function of GnRH is indeed to initiate the cascade required for puberty by stimulating the production of luteinizing hormone (LH) and follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH), both essential for sexual maturation processes. Unlike insulin, leptin, or growth hormone, whose main actions pertain more broadly to metabolism regulation rather than directly influencing the initiation stages of puberty, GnRH stands out as pivotal in this context.
